what_it_does: This file is a noticeboard block item that documents the root cause of a chat-send failure and explains why replies to a non-registered human originator named 'tim' cannot be pushed.
observations: The /chat-send path only files a board `message` item and does not call `cc_channels.send`
`route_reply` pushes replies only when the originator is a live channel session with a port
'tim' is described as a human on a phone, not a registered session
Replies to 'tim' are recorded in the mail log but cannot be pushed because there is no port
The item is linked as `part_of` board://item-90a639e7
standouts: Pinpoints the missing call `cc_channels.send` as the whole bug
Highlights that replies to unregistered human originators are logged but not pushable
Frames the app itself as the required endpoint for replies to 'tim'
conventions: Uses noticeboard YAML front matter with id, address, type, source, state, title, links, and history
Markdown body uses bullet points for diagnosis
References board URIs and channel session identifiers
Title uses an 'R2' prefix suggesting a numbered reasoning/response item
concerns: Diagnostic claims reference `/chat-send`, `cc_channels.send`, and `route_reply` without showing code in this file
The identity and schema for 'tim' are not defined here
The statement 'the app must BE that endpoint' is asserted without implementation detail
notes: A diagnostic noticeboard item explaining that chat messages are not injected into channels and replies to the unregistered human 'tim' have no push endpoint.
questions: What is the exact contract and side effect of `cc_channels.send`?
How is 'tim' represented in the data model?
What distinguishes a live channel session from a registered session?
What does it mean concretely for the app to 'BE that endpoint'?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches content: it diagnoses why chat-send fails and why 'tim' is unreachable.
intention_signals: Document the bug and the delivery constraint so the next implementation routes chat through the channel layer and treats the app as the endpoint for replies to unregistered humans.
novelty: It explicitly distinguishes filing a board message from injecting it into a channel, and logging a reply from pushing it to a live port.
contribution: Keeps the diagnosis that chat-send must invoke a channel send path and that replies to non-session humans require the app to act as the endpoint.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard diagnostic item stating that /chat-send fails to inject messages into channels because it only files a board item, and replies to the unregistered human 'tim' cannot be pushed because route_reply requires a live channel session port.
intention_for_embedding: To record why the current chat path fails and why replies to 'tim' are undeliverable so the fix can route messages through the channel layer and make the app the human endpoint.
